channel.title: Biology on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Biology on emsenn.net

item.title: Morphogenesis
item.description: Morphogenesis — the generation of form — is one of the central problems in biology and one of its most direct connections to relational thinking. How does a fertilized egg, a single cell, become a structured organism with differentiated tissues, organs, and body plans? The standard molecular account attributes form to genetic programs: DNA encodes instructions, and development executes them. But this account faces a persistent difficulty. The genome specifies proteins, not shapes. The gap between molecular components and macroscopic form requires an account of how relational organization emerges from local interactions.
